text: # Self-employed people in childcare are back in paid employment en masse

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: [Analysis](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/analyses/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: [Business services](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/zakelijk-diensten/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: [Mario Bersem](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/authors/mario-bersem/index.html)

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: In childcare, self-employed people without employees (self-employed people) will return to paid employment in large numbers in 2025, usually directly with childcare organizations but also through employment agencies and secondment companies. The use of self-employed people in day care and out-of-school care (BSO) will decrease by 70 percent, ABN AMRO estimates based on a survey of childcare organizations, trade associations and self-employed mediators.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Where childcare organizations previously could not avoid self-employed persons, they now work with their own staff and with temporary workers, i.e. people with an employment contract. “We will not have employed a self-employed person in 2025,” says Remko Berkel, CEO of Babilou Family, a childcare organization with three hundred locations.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: It is a remarkable turnaround for the sector, where in 2024 an average of around 10 percent of childcare was provided by 17,000 self-employed people. Converted, out of the total of 900,000 children who went to childcare, an average of 90,000 children were in the care of self-employed persons, which equates to approximately 6 million childcare hours per month (see diagram).

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: The change in the childcare labor market is the result of the tax authorities; which has maintained bogus self-employment since January 1, 2025 on the basis of the DBA Act. The risk of bogus self-employment is high in childcare, healthcare, education and in the government itself, as ABN AMRO previously described in the report 'Employers struggle with enforcing bogus self-employment'.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: [Read also: Employers struggle with enforcement of bogus self-employment](/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/sectorprognoses/alle-sectoren/employers-struggling-with-handhaving-schijnself-employment.html)

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: ## Risk of false self-employment is high in childcare

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Pedagogical workers (PM'ers) who work with children 'in the group' run a great risk of false self-employment when they do this as a self-employed person, as shown, for example, in the publication ZZP - yes or no? of the tax authorities. In this publication, a PM in childcare serves as an example of someone who is not self-employed due to the high degree of embeddedness and management within the organization. This assessment is guided by the Deliveroo ruling from 2023, in which the Supreme Court has provided nine points of view to determine whether there is an employment contract (employee) or a commission agreement (self-employed person).

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Due to the high risk of false self-employment, the childcare sector has said that it no longer wants to employ self-employed people in the group. In anticipation of the enforcement of bogus self-employment, Babilou Family was the first major childcare organization to publicly announce in July 2024 that it would no longer work with self-employed people: “If you follow the law, it is impossible to be self-employed in childcare,” Berkel told NOS at the time. Many other childcare organizations followed, as did the trade associations.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: ## But self-employed people will continue to be active in childcare after January 1, 2025

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: However, some self-employed people will continue to work in the childcare sector in 2025 and beyond. For example, in childcare (see box 'Childcare in figures'), where relatively many independent childminders are active, around 7,500, and where there is no false self-employment. But also in day care and out-of-school care, working on a self-employed basis remains possible on a small scale, for example in specialist services such as music lessons or sports lessons and probably also in emergency substitute services.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Tadaah, an online platform for self-employed mediation in childcare, is fully committed to this. Tadaah has drawn up thirteen specific independence criteria for childcare, based on the aforementioned nine viewpoints of the Supreme Court. “If these criteria are met, for example in the event of an emergency, stand-in services that are announced very shortly in advance, then PMs can still work as a self-employed person,” says Steven Lenderink, general manager of Tadaah. Lenderink regularly sees people who combine paid employment with entrepreneurship via the platform: “they combine the security of a job with the autonomy of a self-employed person”.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: ## Transition labor market also visible in other sectors

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: The reason for the childcare sector to largely ban self-employed persons was the enforcement of bogus self-employment by the tax authorities. But the industry had more reasons to want to get rid of self-employed people, as expressed by the largest industry associations BK and BMK:

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: "The use of self-employed people 'in the group' does not contribute to the continuity of work and the emotional safety of children. In addition, it creates crooked faces among permanent staff. Self-employed people have fewer tasks than permanent employees, but are paid better, so the perception is. Finally, the use of self-employed people drives up the cost price, which puts pressure on the accessibility of childcare."

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: The use of self-employed people is also controversial in healthcare and education. Lack of continuity, concerns about collegiality and high costs also play a role there, as does the risk of false self-employment. Noël de Vries, CEO of healthcare secondment company TMI, has seen a sharp decrease in the number of applications for self-employed persons in healthcare since January 1, 2025. “Childcare is at the forefront, but you see this transition throughout healthcare,” says de Vries. At the same time, the number of applications on a temporary or secondment basis is actually increasing. The total capacity is not decreasing, de Vries thinks: “People are not so quick to leave healthcare to start again somewhere else.”

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Childcare had six thousand vacancies in the fourth quarter of 2024, according to figures from the Central Bureau of Statistics (CBS). The labor market for childcare is above average tight, with 130,000 employed people and a vacancy rate of 46. But ABN AMRO expects that staff shortages in childcare will not increase significantly due to the maintenance of bogus self-employment. Firstly, because self-employed people have largely returned to paid employment. In addition, childcare organizations more often work with temporary workers, a group that was previously rarely used in the sector. And finally, childcare organizations have made adjustments that make their employees willing to work more. They do this, for example, by scheduling smarter, with more attention to the wishes of their own staff.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Larger childcare organizations have also expanded their flexible pools, with their own staff, to better meet the desire for autonomy and variation of some of their staff. Childcare Morgen, with forty locations in the The Hague region, has seized the urgency of January 1, 2025 to offer more choice to its own staff, says director Tineke Onink. An extensive and central flexible pool bundles supply and demand from all locations and expands capacity. Onink says he is happy with the tax authorities' measures: "it has brought clarity and stimulated positive change."

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: The childcare sector cares for around 900,000 children and thus increases the labor participation of parents, according to the 'quarterly Childcare Reports' of the Ministry of Social Affairs and Employment (SZW). The small number of self-employed people who have left the industry has been compensated by an increase in the work of people who have become employed and also of employees who were already employed. This does not alter the fact that staff shortages in childcare are still a challenge and may lead to some groups remaining closed. Staff shortages are an important business risk, just like in other parts of healthcare and the economy.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: The childcare offering consists of day care (0-4 years), which approximately 383,000 children use monthly, and out-of-school care (4-12 years), for approximately 458,000 children. In addition, around 75,000 children go to a paid childminder. In total, 916,000 children in the Netherlands go to daycare with childcare allowance every month, according to figures from the quarterly childcare report of SZW.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: On average, children who go to daycare go about two days a week. For day care this means 91.6 hours on average per month; for out-of-school care the average is 43.1 hours per month. In total, the industry provides almost 60 million hours of child care per month: day care represents 59 percent of the total; after-school care 33 percent; and childcare 8 percent (see diagram).

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: ## The number of self-employed people is also decreasing due to shrinkage of childcare

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: There are relatively many self-employed people in childcare, who, however, run little risk of false self-employment because they usually work at home and for themselves; that is, at your own risk and expense and without embedding or direction. Of the 17,000,000 self-employed people working in childcare at the end of 2024, an estimated 7,500 worked as childminders. The above analysis about the sharp decline in the use of self-employed people in childcare mainly concerns day care and out-of-school care, which together represent 92 percent of childcare.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: The number of self-employed people in childcare is also decreasing, but for a different reason. Childminder care has shrunk sharply in the last five years, by 40 percent, while day care and out-of-school care have grown, by 12 percent and 17 percent respectively. In April 2025, the government announced plans to [reverse the decline in the number of childminders](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/actueel/nieuws/2025/04/10/kabinet-komen-met-measures-om-gastouderopvang-aantrekkelijker-te-maken). Moreover, the government still plans to make [entire childcare almost free](https://www.rijksoverheid.nl/actueel/nieuws/2025/04/25/kabinet-zet-nieuwe-stap-naar-bijna-gratis-kinderopvang-voor-werken-ouders) for working parents, which is expected to significantly increase the demand for childcare.

metadata.source_title: Self-employed people in childcare are once again employed en masse

text: Business Services is the lubricant of the Dutch knowledge economy. New ways of working are often the first to be applied in this sector, making it an important driver in the transition to a circular economy.
